title: Lone Contact
subtitle: Passive Sonar Operations Trainer

briefing.situation: A single unescorted RED merchant is transiting sector ECHO-4 on a steady easterly course at roughly 6 knots. There is nothing else in the water. Conditions are calm with no surface-duct or layer effects — a clean acoustic picture for training.
briefing.mission: Learn the fundamentals of passive sonar operation: detect, classify, track a bearing, and — without going active — solve the contact's range using Target Motion Analysis (TMA).
briefing.execution: Rig for passive. Active sonar is secured for the duration (EMCON). Find the contact on the broadband display, designate it, and keep a bearing log. The contact's bearing will draw across your bow — that bearing rate is your raw material. Run one tracking leg, then maneuver own-ship and run a second leg; the change in bearing rate gives you range. When your solution is stable you may close for a firing solution.
briefing.threats: None. The merchant is unarmed, unescorted, and unaware of you. There is no time pressure and no penalty for taking your time — the only objective is to learn the sensor.
briefing.notes: This is a pure sonar trainer. Do not use active sonar — the whole point is to range the contact passively. Follow the SONAR coaching messages as they arrive; they walk you through detect → classify → track → TMA → solution.

red_mission_summary: A lone RED cargo vessel transits east to its waypoint at a steady course and speed. It has no weapons, no escort, and no awareness of the submarine. It does not evade.
blue_mission_summary: BLUE submarine practices passive sonar fundamentals against a single clean contact: detection, classification, bearing tracking, and passive ranging via Target Motion Analysis. EMCON is enforced as a training discipline.
objective: Detect, track, and passively range the lone contact using TMA without using active sonar.
blue_captain_summary: Sonar training mission. BLUE will detect the single RED merchant (red-01), classify it, maintain a bearing log, and solve its range by TMA — running a tracking leg, maneuvering own-ship, and running a second leg. Maintain EMCON (no active sonar). Optional capstone: once a stable solution exists, a single passive-launched torpedo on red-01 completes the exercise.
roe: EMCON: active sonar is secured for the entire exercise.
Weapons release authorized only after a stable passive range solution exists.
No time pressure — prioritize a clean solution over speed.

BLUE.narrative: A single unescorted merchant is crossing the bow on a steady easterly course. The water is otherwise empty and the acoustic picture is clean — ideal conditions to learn the passive sonar workflow end to end.
BLUE.primary_objective: Passively range the contact via TMA without going active
BLUE.win_condition: Establish a stable range solution; optionally engage to confirm it
BLUE.lose_condition: None — this is a training exercise
BLUE.intelligence: One contact only. Unarmed merchant, ~6 knots, no escort, unaware of own-ship.
constraints: Run a tracking leg, then maneuver, then a second leg

RED.narrative: Single cargo vessel in routine transit toward waypoint [8000, 3000]. No escort, no threat awareness. Hold course and speed.
RED.intelligence: No reported submarine activity in this sector.

ship_behaviors.red-01: You are red-01, a lone unarmed cargo vessel in routine transit. Hold a steady course of 090 and a steady speed of 6 knots toward your waypoint at X:8000, Y:3000. You have no weapons, no escort, and no awareness of any submarine. Do NOT evade, zig-zag, or change speed — maintain a predictable steady track. Predictability is correct for this transit.

triggers.comms: SONAR TRAINER: Rig for passive. Active sonar is secured for this exercise (EMCON). Watch the broadband display and report any contact by bearing.
Conn, Sonar: new broadband contact bearing ~315, designate Sierra-1. Start a bearing log and mark a bearing every 30 seconds.
Sonar: Sierra-1 holds a slow, steady blade rate — single screw. Classify probable merchant, roughly 6 knots.
Conn: Sierra-1's bearing is drawing right. A steady bearing means closing; a drawing bearing means it will pass. One bearing alone gives you direction, not range.
TMA STEP 1: You're on your first leg. Hold this course and keep logging bearings — this leg establishes a baseline bearing rate.
TMA STEP 2: Now maneuver to change the geometry. Recommend come right to course 090, ahead two-thirds, and steady for three minutes.
TMA STEP 3: Second leg established. Compare the bearing rate before and after your turn — the change in rate is what resolves range (Ekelund solution).
Sonar: solution converging. Cross-check your range estimate against the contact's behavior; refine your course to slowly close the range.
Conn: when your range solution is stable and you hold a clean bearing on Sierra-1, you are weapons-free. Passive launch only — do NOT go active.
